        "message": "You are Nova helping Massimo keep a lightweight end-of-day reflection habit. Your goal is to help him capture just a couple of useful bullet points. Ask for a brief reflection in one compact message with two parts only: 1) 2-4 bullet points about what he did / what mattered today, and 2) 2-4 bullet points for tomorrow's priorities or intentions. Keep it lightweight, practical, and easy to answer. Before finishing, if today's daily memory exists append a marker under a dedicated section named exactly '## Daily reflection' that the reflection prompt was sent; if the file does not exist, create it and add that section with the marker. Mention that you'll store the answers there too. Load the previous few days so you can lightly keep continuity and avoid repetitive prompts, but do not over-summarize them.",

        "thinking": "low",
        "timeoutSeconds": 180
      },

      "name": "Daily AI agents digest (PH + HN)",

      "schedule": {
        "kind": "cron",
        "expr": "0 8 * * *",

      "sessionTarget": "isolated",
      "wakeMode": "now",
      "payload": {
        "kind": "agentTurn",
        "message": "Produce a concise daily digest of the most relevant launches and news related to AI agents from the last 24 hours, using only Product Hunt and Hacker News as sources. Search the web for the last 24 hours. Prioritize genuinely important or interesting items: launches, product releases, major open-source agent tools, notable demos, benchmark/eval discussions tied to agent products, and significant discussions that indicate real momentum. Ignore generic AI news unless it is clearly agent-related. Output format:\n1) A short title line: 'AI agents digest — <date>'\n2) 3-7 bullets, each with: item name/topic, why it matters in one sentence, and source label(s) with link(s)\n3) A final 'Watchlist' line with 1-3 things that look promising but uncertain\nKeep it tight, useful, and non-hypey. If there is little of substance, say so plainly and include only the few worthwhile items.",
        "model": "gpt",
        "thinking": "low",
        "timeoutSeconds": 240
      },
      "delivery": {

      "sessionTarget": "isolated",
      "wakeMode": "now",
      "payload": {
        "kind": "agentTurn",
        "message": "Morning health summary. Do these steps in order:\n\n1. LOAD SLEEP HISTORY: Read the last 7 daily memory files in ~/.openclaw/workspace/memory/ (YYYY-MM-DD.md, most recent first). Extract any \"## Sleep\" section from each to identify trends in score, deep sleep, REM, and efficiency.\n\n2. DELIVER MORNING SUMMARY: Follow the Morning Summary Template from your oura skill exactly. After the standard summary block, add one trend line based on step 1 — e.g. \"Sleep trend: improving — up 8 pts from your 7-day avg\" (skip if no history yet).\n\n3. SAVE TO MEMORY: Append a \"## Sleep\" section to ~/.openclaw/workspace/memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md (today's actual date, create file if missing) with these bullet points: score, total sleep, deep, REM, light, efficiency, lowest HR, avg HR, avg HRV, bedtime, wake time.",
        "timeoutSeconds": 180
      },
      "delivery": {
        "mode": "announce",
        "channel": "telegram",
        "to": "437587256"
      },

        "message": "Run the daily reading digest. Follow the reading-list skill exactly:\n\n1. Load ~/.openclaw/workspace/reading-list/links.json and ~/.openclaw/workspace/reading-list/digest-history.json.\n2. Load ~/.openclaw/workspace/reading-list/interests.md (create it from memory if missing — search memory for topics, projects, and interests the user has).\n3. Select the best candidate links using the scoring rules in the skill.\n4. Fetch each candidate and evaluate whether it is genuinely interesting.\n5. Apply the anti-spam rules: skip if fewer than 2 interesting links, or if there has been a digest 3 days in a row, or if all candidates were recently covered.\n6. If sending: compose the digest in the format from the skill and send via Telegram.\n7. Update links.json (increment times_included, last_included) and append to digest-history.json.\n8. Do not send anything if skipping — just log the skip reason to digest-history.json.",

      },
